data.title: Broadcom stock falls despite strong earnings, with a $73B AI backlog. Analysts raise price targets.
data.description: Broadcom Inc's stock prices plunged in early trading on Friday, despite the company's positive fiscal fourth-quarter earnings report. Analysts are attributing AVGO's decline to various factors. BofA Securities analyst Vivek Arya maintained a Buy rating and raised the price target to $500 from $460. Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider also upheld a Buy rating, raising the price target to $450 from $435. Cody Acree from Benchmark reaffirmed a Buy rating and raised the price target to $485 from $385. Additionally, Kevin Cassidy from Rosenblatt Securities reiterated a Buy rating, with the price target increasing from $440 to $450. BofA Securities highlighted Broadcom's AI backlogs of $73 billion for the next six months, exceeding consensus estimates of $69 billion. The backlog and order visibility are steadily increasing, with management hinting at a range of $50-$100 billion in potential orders. Other analysts also provided their stock ratings on the matter.

data.title: First Brands Group, LLC reveals business stabilization efforts with ordering at 90% of pre-petition levels and 50 new opportunities.
data.description: A prominent aftermarket automotive parts supplier, First Brands Group, LLC, based in Rochester Hills, Michigan, revealed recent strategic actions to enhance its operations and leadership. These changes aim to ensure a consistent and reliable flow of products and services to customers. The company has witnessed a notable increase in demand, with orders reaching over 90% compared to pre-petition levels. Additionally, they have secured more than 50 opportunities for new business, showcasing a positive trajectory in customer engagement and market expansion.

data.title: HCIactive enhances SmartBenefits.ai with new AI-driven features for member experience, focusing on clarity and decision support.
data.description: HCIactive, based in Columbia, Maryland, has revealed significant upgrades to their member experience platform, SmartBenefits.ai. This AI-powered platform aims to streamline benefits processes, such as value accumulation, eligibility, claims navigation, and personalized guidance. The enhancements align with HCIactive’s commitment to prioritizing AI in all aspects of their operations and enhancing the member experience. The latest upgrades focus on a user-centric approach to increase clarity, reduce obstacles, and provide improved real-time decision support. Notable improvements include a...

data.title: Gilead Sciences-partnered Arcus Biosciences stocks plummeted, reversing 287% gain, due to failed cancer regimen.
data.description: The stock of Arcus Biosciences experienced a significant drop on Friday, erasing a previous gain of almost 300%, following the failure of its cancer treatment collaboration with Gilead Sciences. This reversal marked an end to the company's eight-month winning streak, causing a notable impact on its financial standing.

data.title: Newmont stock in buy zone due to record silver prices, fell slightly as silver reversed from highs.
data.description: The stock of Newmont saw a surge on Thursday, entering a buy range due to the sharp increase in silver prices. However, it experienced a slight decline on Friday as silver prices retreated from their peak levels. The record-high prices of silver pushed the mining giant into a favorable buy zone, highlighting the company's potential for growth and investment opportunities, as discussed in the Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Oracle stock drops due to delay in OpenAI data centers to 2028, as reported by Bloomberg.
data.description: The stock value of Oracle decreased following a report from Bloomberg stating that certain data centers being developed for OpenAI by the company will not be completed until 2028. This delay caused a further decline in Oracle's stock price.

data.title: Investors sell off Broadcom stock despite beat and raise report for fiscal Q4, disappointing some despite positive results.
data.description: Investors reacted nervously to Broadcom's fiscal Q4 results and guidance, causing a sell-off in the company's stock. Despite beating estimates, the stock declined. The reason for the drop is unclear, but it may be due to profit-taking or concerns about future growth. Overall, Broadcom remains financially strong, reporting impressive numbers for the quarter.

data.title: EMA committee proposes expanding Eli Lilly's drug Mounjaro to kids aged 10, boosting company's growth.
data.description: A committee from the EMA suggested on Friday to expand the approval of Eli Lilly's type 2 diabetes medication, Mounjaro, to include children aged 10 and above. Following this recommendation, Eli Lilly's stock rose, reflecting positive investor sentiment.

data.title: Investment banker Arthur L. Carter, 93, dies. He purchased The Nation magazine & founded The New York Observer.
data.description: Following his success on Wall Street, he acquired The Nation magazine and established The New York Observer, described by a writer as a hub of Manhattan gossip and intrigue.

data.title: Bitnomial granted CFTC approval to launch prediction markets by offering fully-collateralized swaps, as of Dec. 12, 2025.
data.description: Bitnomial, a U.S. derivatives exchange company based in Chicago, disclosed on December 12, 2025, that its subsidiary Bitnomial Clearinghouse, LLC has been granted approval by the CFTC to clear fully-collateralized swaps. This development paves the way for Bitnomial to introduce prediction markets. This move signifies a significant step forward for Bitnomial in expanding its services, with implications for the financial market.
